hero.subtitle: Corporate administration and governance, delivered with accountability end-to-end.

about.title: Corporate Administration & Governance Services
paragraphs: Axsuma is a UK-based provider of corporate administration and governance services. We support businesses, family offices, funds, and their advisors with their administration needs at every stage of the company lifecycle, from entity formation through company secretarial, M&A deal support, accounting and tax compliance, and payroll in the United Kingdom.

Accountability drives everything we do. We combine institutional-grade technical expertise with pragmatic, hands-on delivery. Each assignment has a named owner, decisions are shaped by commercial realities, and work is executed thoroughly and on schedule.

We do not provide legal advice or structuring. Instead, we work closely with clients' lawyers and accountants to ensure decisions are implemented cleanly, accurately and without friction. Our role is to take ownership of execution — translating professional advice into precise, dependable delivery.

sectors.title: Businesses & Corporates
sectors.description: We support companies of all sizes, from start-ups to multinational corporations, ensuring statutory and regulatory obligations are met efficiently while management teams focus on growth and strategy.

sectors.title: Family Offices
sectors.description: We provide family offices with bespoke governance, entity management, and reporting solutions. Simplifying administrative complexity to help families protect wealth and maintain control over their affairs.

sectors.title: Funds & Investment Vehicles
sectors.description: We help investment funds and special purpose vehicles navigate corporate administration and compliance with confidence, from entity formation to ongoing reporting.

sectors.title: Advisors & Professional Partners
sectors.description: We work closely with lawyers, accountants, and other advisors to deliver a seamless service to their clients, taking responsibility for operational execution while ensuring excellence is maintained.

sectors.title: International Law Firms
sectors.description: When your clients need complex cross-border structures delivered compliantly and at pace, we become an extension of your team.

contact.title: Let's Talk
contact.body: Our experts are always happy to talk. Please get in touch and we will connect you with the appropriate specialist and reply within two working days.

header.title: About Axsuma
header.subtitle: UK-based provider of corporate administration and governance services, supporting ambitious investors and enterprises.

paragraphs: Axsuma is a UK-based provider of corporate administration and governance services. We support businesses, family offices, funds, and their advisors with their administration needs at every stage of the company lifecycle, from entity formation through company secretarial, M&A deal support, accounting and tax compliance, and payroll in the United Kingdom.

sections.title: What we do
sections.content: We help our clients meet statutory and regulatory obligations with confidence, while enhancing operational efficiency. By implementing robust governance frameworks, we enable sustainable growth and long-term value creation. Our services are designed to provide clarity and control throughout every stage of a company's lifecycle.

sections.title: How we are different
sections.content: Accountability drives everything we do. We combine institutional-grade technical expertise with pragmatic, hands-on delivery. Each assignment has a named owner, decisions are shaped by commercial realities, and work is executed thoroughly and on schedule. We prioritise clarity, control and consistent follow-through, the factors that make a tangible difference to our clients.
sections.content_2: We do not provide legal advice or structuring. Instead, we work closely with clients' lawyers and accountants to ensure decisions are implemented cleanly, accurately and without friction. Our role is to take ownership of execution. Translating professional advice into precise, dependable delivery.

sections.title: Why Choose Axsuma?
sections.intro: We combine human judgement with structured processes to deliver clarity, accountability, and dependable outcomes. Our sector expertise allows us to anticipate challenges, remove friction, and provide peace of mind across every mandate.

cards.title: Speed & Efficiency
cards.text: Streamlined processes and institutional expertise enable us to deliver outcomes faster, without compromising quality or attention to detail.

cards.title: Partnership Approach
cards.text: We embed with our clients, working closely with their teams, advisors, and stakeholders to ensure seamless execution and alignment.

cards.title: Compliance & Governance
cards.text: Institutional-grade expertise ensures every mandate meets statutory obligations and regulatory requirements with precision and accountability.

header.title: Our Approach
header.subtitle: How we deliver corporate administration services focused on responsibility, transparency, and dependable outcomes.

paragraphs: Our clients and business partners come to us with challenges that carry real consequences. Getting it wrong is not an option. We exist for the moments that matter when decisions have impact, timelines are tight, and ambiguity is unacceptable.

While the industry talks about process, platforms, and coverage, we focus on what truly matters to clients: judgement, accountability, and delivery. Corporate services have drifted into fragmentation. Work is distributed, ownership is unclear, and clients are left exposed. Our approach is deliberately designed to close that gap.

services.title: We Take Responsibility
services.content: Not shared. Not diluted. Not buried in a workflow. We take clear, human ownership of every mandate from the first instruction to the final outcome. Someone is always accountable and nothing disappears into a system. When complexity arises, we step in, take control, and keep work moving.

services.title: Transparency in Pricing
services.content: Our approach to fees is grounded in transparency, accountability, and commercial alignment. All costs are agreed upfront with fixed fees applied to clearly defined workstreams wherever possible. This provides clients with full cost certainty, mitigates the risk of unexpected overruns, and enables effective management of overall spend.

services.title: We Finish What We Start
services.content: Delivery is not a phase. It is the outcome. We stay until the job is done properly, on time, without friction, and without dropped balls. No fuss. No theatre. Just work done well.

closing.tagline: Not the biggest.Not the loudest.The most dependable.

header.title: Meet the Team
header.subtitle: Our founders and senior team bring decades of experience from leading institutions combined with a relationship-first approach.

paragraphs: Our founders and senior team bring decades of experience from Magic Circle law firms, Big 4 accountancy practices and leading corporate service providers. They combine institutional expertise with a relationship-first approach, so you get technically robust, regulator-ready advice delivered with commercial sense and personal accountability.

members.name: Jason Reader
bio: Jason is the founder and Managing Director – Operations, with over 30 years' experience in the Corporate Services sector.

Jason has led UK operational entities, overseeing company formations, secretariat functions, legal process agent services, registers of overseas entities, and client acceptance and onboarding for regulated services.

He supports complex global business structures and drives client outcomes, including supporting UK directorships for both short-term transactional objectives and longer-term non-executive or fractional roles.

members.name: Charlotte Phipps-Hornby
bio: Charlotte has over 20 years' experience in international professional services. She advises VC- and PE-backed growth companies, multinationals, and asset managers on cross-border expansion, governance, compliance, and complex transactions.

Throughout her career, she has developed and led international teams and strategic initiatives across sectors including corporate and global expansion, private wealth, and private equity. Having held senior leadership roles at leading corporate service providers, including Vistra, CSC Intertrust, and ZEDRA, she combines strong commercial acumen with deep technical expertise to deliver scalable, high-quality solutions for international clients. Prior to this, she worked at Allen & Overy in their Regulatory & Investment Funds group.

members.name: Sarah Youll
bio: Sarah is a Chartered Accountant and Tax Advisor who trained as a Chartered Accountant with big 4 firm, PwC. She then went on to be a Director for an accounting firm with over 500 clients in the hospitality industry. Sarah has gained substantial corporate service experience whilst working for Vistra.
Sarah is passionate about working with business owners and their management teams providing financial and operational advice that supports their business to succeed on every level.

members.name: Gurpreet Singh
bio: Gurpreet is a strategic HR leader and trusted advisor who works closely with founders, executives, and leadership teams across high-growth and international businesses. With experience spanning enterprise technology, SaaS, and private equity-backed environments, she focuses on building practical, scalable people strategies that drive performance, engagement, and long-term growth.
She brings a strong balance of strategic thinking and hands-on delivery, with a commercial and pragmatic approach.

members.name: Martin Palmer
bio: Martin has worked in the UK and international trust and corporate services sector for nearly 40 years.
He has a law degree from Nottingham University, and in 2012 was awarded the Advanced Diploma in International Taxation from the UK Chartered Institute of Taxation.

He has spoken and written widely on corporate law and international tax matters since the mid 1990's. His written output includes articles for the Journal of International Tax Trust & Corporate Planning and a number of the Special Supplements of the well known legal textbook Gore Browne on Companies. He has written a book entitled 'UK Companies: Tax Advantages for International Business' now in its 5th Edition, published by Lexis Nexis.

members.name: Joanne Reader
bio: Joanne is a qualified accountant who has operated in client accounting roles and in-house finance management roles. Joanne has a wealth of experience in Payroll, bookkeeping and VAT gained through her previous roles, including previous corporate services experience with Jordans.
Joanne is a dedicated member of the team and always on hand to support clients in respect of their finance, payroll, bookkeeping and VAT queries.

header.title: Company Formation Services
header.subtitle: Expert UK company incorporation and establishment. From Limited Companies to LLPs, we handle everything under the Companies Act 2006.

overview.title: Complete UK Company Formation Solutions
paragraphs: Axsuma provides a complete solution, whether you are seeking a straightforward private limited company incorporation or need additional support for a bespoke entity or complex SPV. We guide you through every stage of formation—from name availability verification through to post-incorporation compliance and tax registration.

We specialise in navigating the Companies House registration process, managing articles of association, establishing company governance, and ensuring you meet all statutory obligations from incorporation onwards. Our team has processed hundreds of incorporations, from straightforward single-director companies to complex multi-shareholder corporate structures.

Every company we form is registered with Companies House under the Companies Act 2006 and comes with proper documentation, statutory registers, and a clear governance framework tailored to your business needs.

services.title: Private Limited Companies
paragraphs: A private company limited by shares is the most common business structure in the UK, offering its owners limited liability protection. UK private limited companies are a popular vehicle for operational businesses as well as being commonly used as Holding Companies or SPVs for specific transactions.

When you incorporate with Axsuma, we handle every detail: from submitting your incorporation documents to Companies House through to delivering your completed incorporation package. The incorporation process begins with choosing a unique company name that complies with Companies House naming rules and is not already registered. We verify name availability, ensure compliance with the Sensitive Words List, and confirm trade mark clearance.

Your memorandum and articles of association are prepared bespoke to your structure, outlining shareholdings, director powers, shareholder rights, voting procedures, and dividend distributions in line with the Companies Act 2006. A company can be incorporated using the Model Articles or with Axsuma's standard Articles.

We prepare all required statutory documents including the application for incorporation (Form IN01), confirmation of the company secretary (if applicable), statement of initial officers, and statement of capital and initial shareholdings. Our incorporations include setup of statutory registers (members, directors, secretaries, charges, and persons with significant control), issuance of share certificates, and registration for corporation tax with HMRC. We ensure your company is fully tax-registered and ready for trading immediately upon incorporation.

services.title: Ready-Made Company Stock
paragraphs: For businesses that need to operate immediately, ready-made companies offer instant trading capability. Axsuma holds a stock of UK ready-made private limited companies that can be made available and reserved for urgent transactions. You acquire the company through a transfer of shares, change directors and shareholders through standard resolutions, and commence trading the same day.

Ready-made companies are ideal for fast-moving ventures, time-sensitive acquisitions, or situations where waiting for Companies House processing is commercially unviable. The company arrives fully registered with Companies House, with a current Confirmation Statement, and registered with HMRC for corporation tax and PAYE. You simply provide your director, shareholder, and registered office details, we update the registers, and you're live.

We hold a stock of single stand-alone entities or stacks, comprising of Hold Co, Mid Co and Bid Co specifically for use with highly confidential Merger and Acquisition transactions. Each ready-made company includes a clean cap table, blank statutory registers, and a resignation letter from the existing director. The transfer process is completed within hours, making ready-made companies perfect for time-critical mandates such as immediate trading requirements, acquisition closings, or regulatory submissions under tight deadlines.

All ready-made companies comply fully with the Companies Act 2006 and Companies House requirements, with no hidden liabilities or historical baggage. You're acquiring a clean, dormant company structure ready for your business operations.

services.title: Limited Liability Partnerships (LLPs)
paragraphs: The UK Limited Liability Partnership (LLP) provides a UK vehicle that has the tax status and flexibility of a partnership, but with a separate legal personality and limited liability for its members. Unlike traditional partnerships, LLP members enjoy limited liability protection—you're not personally liable for the negligence or misconduct of other members.

LLP formation mirrors company incorporation but with distinct statutory requirements. We prepare the incorporation application alongside the LLP agreement. All new UK LLPs will need to operate under the provisions set out in the LLP Regulations (Regulation 7), however, Axsuma recommends that a specific LLP operating agreement is adopted. The LLP agreement defines member contributions, profit-sharing arrangements, management rights, member exit procedures, and dispute resolution.

The incorporation process involves submitting incorporation documents to Companies House, registration of initial members, and filing the LLP agreement. Upon completion, you'll receive confirmation of the LLP's registration number and incorporation certificate. We then register the LLP with HMRC for corporation tax and any relevant regulatory bodies.

We ensure your LLP is fully established, properly governed, and registered with all relevant authorities from day one. LLPs must file annual accounts and confirmation statements with Companies House, though they benefit from certain filing exemptions available to smaller LLPs.

cta.title: Ready to Incorporate Your UK Company?

header.subtitle: Specialized structuring for SPVs, holding companies, and complex investment vehicles

paragraphs: We provide end-to-end support for the creation and administration of UK Special Purpose Vehicles (SPVs) and UK holding companies. Whether you are structuring an investment, executing a transaction, acquiring UK assets, or establishing a long-term corporate presence, we deliver compliant, efficient, and professionally managed corporate structures tailored to your objectives.

Our services ensure that your UK entity is properly formed, fully compliant with local statutory obligations, and supported by experienced Axsuma directors who understand the demands of cross-border investments and regulated transactions.

services.title: UK SPVs
paragraphs: UK SPVs are widely used for asset ownership, financing structures, securitisations, joint ventures, and risk-isolation strategies. We establish SPVs quickly and in line with UK regulatory and commercial requirements.
subheadings: We specialise in SPVs for:

services.title: UK Holding Companies
paragraphs: A UK holding company offers a stable legal environment, access to a comprehensive double tax treaty network, and clear corporate governance standards. We assist domestic and international clients in establishing holding companies for investment, consolidation, or strategic ownership purposes.

The use of UK companies as holding companies is an attractive proposition given the extensive double tax treaties available can significantly reduce any impact to dividend withholding tax and further strengthened with the introduction of the Substantial Shareholder Exemption rules.

header.title: Company Secretarial & Governance
header.subtitle: Governance support, statutory compliance, board administration, and registered office services

paragraphs: The role of company secretaries, in-house legal teams, and compliance teams is becoming increasingly complex. We support our clients as an extension of their team, whenever and however they need us. This could be interim support during busy periods, specialist project assistance, a fully outsourced company secretarial function, or tailored support to suit your specific needs.

Our company secretarial services ensure your company remains compliant with the Companies Act 2006, Companies House filing obligations, and corporate governance best practices. Our team manages the complete framework of statutory obligations, including maintaining registers, filing annual confirmations, managing director changes, handling persons with significant control (PSC) notifications, and keeping corporate records current and compliant.

We can be formally appointed as your corporate Company Secretary or provide flexible company secretarial support. Whether you are a small private company or a larger corporate structure, our services adapt to meet your needs.

Every company must have a registered office for statutory notices and legal correspondence. Axsuma provides a prestigious London address that appears on all Companies House filings, public documents, and statutory notices. We receive all statutory correspondence, including documents from Companies House (such as confirmation statement reminders and annual filing notices) and HMRC communications (tax assessments, penalties, enquiries).
Our mail forwarding service includes email notifications with scanned copies of all mail received, ensuring you stay fully informed without the need for a physical office presence.

If your company is currently managed by another provider, Axsuma can seamlessly take over company secretarial support. We coordinate the transition, retrieve all statutory information, verify register accuracy, identify pending filings, and assume responsibility for ongoing governance and compliance. During the transfer-in process, we conduct a comprehensive compliance review: ensuring confirmation statements are up to date, verifying accounts are properly filed, checking director and PSC details for accuracy, and confirming charges are correctly registered. Any gaps or regulatory exposure are addressed with a remediation plan to bring your company fully into compliance.
subheadings: Our Core Company Secretarial Services Include:
Registered Office and Mail Forwarding Services
Transfer-In and Migration from Other Providers

header.title: UK Ready-Made Packages for M&A, Restructuring, and Joint Ventures
header.subtitle: Readiness Solutions for Complex Corporate Transactions

paragraphs: In the fast-paced environment of complex corporate transactions, timing is critical. Complex deal structures, regulatory hurdles, and aggressive timelines mean there is zero margin for administrative delay. We know that as dealmakers, your focus belongs on negotiation, strategy, and execution — not on the distraction of the corporate administration aspects.

Axsuma streamlines the process. We support lawyers, corporates, and private equity firms with pre-established corporate structures purpose-built for fast-moving M&A, restructuring, and carve-outs. We handle the essential administrative groundwork, providing the speed and structural certainty required to close the deal. Beyond completion, we ensure all entities remain fully compliant across their regulatory, accounting, tax, and payroll obligations.

services.title: Transaction-Ready Corporate Stacks
paragraphs: Delays in entity formation, governance setup, or appointing directors can create unnecessary friction during sensitive deal phases. Our solution eliminates these risks.

We offer ready-made HoldCo, MidCo, and BidCo entities available for immediate reservation. These structures are securely held to order exclusively for law firms and advisors, allowing you to focus on deal structuring and negotiation without administrative bottlenecks.

Our fully aligned three-tier acquisition structure is configurable to specific transaction requirements. All entities are established with clean corporate records, dormant status, and are maintained in good standing.

stack_cards.label: HoldCo
stack_cards.description: Ultimate parent entity; structured to support ownership, financing, and long-term governance.

stack_cards.label: MidCo
stack_cards.description: Intermediary holding entity; designed for structural flexibility and risk segregation.

stack_cards.label: BidCo
stack_cards.description: Acquisition vehicle; purpose-built to execute the purchase and receive assets or shares at closing.

services.title: How It Works: Reservation & Hold-to-Order
subheadings: Immediate Reservation
Held to Order for Up to 6 Months (extendable by agreement)
Activation on Demand
paragraphs: Entities can be reserved instantly upon instruction, securing exclusivity for your transaction.
We maintain the corporate stack on your behalf — dormant, compliant, and ready — while deal negotiations progress.
Upon confirmation, entities are fully activated and transferred in accordance with your instructions and transaction timeline.

services.title: Designed for Lawyers & Transaction Advisors
paragraphs: We operate as an infrastructure partner, not a legal decision-maker — supporting your work with speed and precision. Our offering is purpose-built for:
If you are seeking a sole SPV for an upcoming transaction, we also retain standalone entities for immediate reservation on the same held to order basis.

Whether preparing for a competitive bid, conditional signing, or regulatory approval phase, our ready-made HoldCo–MidCo–BidCo stacks give you the structural certainty to move decisively. It will be necessary to conduct some compliance checks in accordance with the UK Money Laundering Regulations upon transfer of ownership at completion.
bullets: Lawyers managing complex and time-sensitive M&A transactions
Private equity and investment advisors
Corporate development and in-house legal teams
Cross-border and multi-jurisdictional deal structures
key_benefits: Reduced time to signing and closing
Flexible holding period aligned with deal timelines

header.title: Accounting & Tax Services
header.subtitle: Unified financial reporting, tax compliance, and accounting support across jurisdictions and corporate structures.

paragraphs: Finance and tax functions are often fragmented across multiple providers, creating inefficiency, inconsistent reporting, and unnecessary administrative burden. We deliver a unified, scalable solution designed to simplify compliance, enhance visibility, and support better financial decision-making across jurisdictions and structures.

services.title: Statutory Accounts & Financial Reporting
paragraphs: We prepare statutory accounts and financial statements in compliance with relevant accounting standards—UK GAAP for smaller entities, IFRS for larger corporates, or consolidated group accounts where required. Every set of accounts includes full notes to the financial statements explaining accounting policies, significant transactions, and director commentary on financial performance and position.

services.title: Corporate Tax Compliance & Advisory
paragraphs: Corporate tax compliance in the UK requires timely filing of corporation tax returns, identification and claiming of available tax reliefs, management of VAT obligations, and coordination with HMRC. We manage all corporation tax filings on behalf of your company, including identifying reliefs such as research and development tax credits, qualifying asset deductions, and loss carry-forward strategies.

services.title: Management Accounts & Financial Analysis
paragraphs: Beyond statutory compliance, we provide tailored management accounts—monthly or quarterly financial reports designed specifically for your business decision-making. These accounts include income statement analysis, balance sheet commentary, cash flow forecasts, and key performance indicators relevant to your business model.

services.title: Outsourced Finance Functions
paragraphs: For growing businesses, outsourced finance functions provide cost-effective alternatives to in-house accounting teams. We handle bookkeeping, payroll administration, VAT compliance, and transaction processing—freeing your team to focus on strategic finance rather than administrative tasks.

why_cards.title: Unified Solutions
why_cards.description: Accounting, tax compliance, and payroll managed by one provider. Eliminate inconsistencies between financial reporting and tax filing—one source of truth across all functions.

why_cards.title: Multi-Jurisdictional Expertise
why_cards.description: Cross-border tax planning, consolidated group reporting, and transfer pricing optimization. Whether your structure spans the UK, Europe, or globally, we coordinate seamless compliance.

why_cards.title: Compliance Certainty
why_cards.description: Timely statutory filings, HMRC correspondence handled professionally, and proactive tax planning. Your compliance obligations are managed with precision and confidence.

header.title: Payroll & HR Services
header.subtitle: End-to-end payroll processing, HMRC compliance, and HR administration support

paragraphs: Payroll and HR management is increasingly complex, driven by evolving legislation, real-time reporting requirements, and heightened expectations around accuracy and employee experience. We provide a streamlined solution that reduces administrative burden while ensuring full compliance with HMRC and employment regulations.

Our approach combines precise payroll delivery with practical HR support, giving you a single, coordinated service that integrates seamlessly with your wider operations. Whether you are managing a growing UK workforce or supporting inbound entities, we deliver consistency, control, and clear visibility.

services.title: Payroll Processing
subheadings: End-to-end payroll delivery, including calculations, payslips, and payment coordination
paragraphs: We handle complete payroll processing, from employee data management through to payment execution and payslip distribution. Our systems integrate with your banking and provide full visibility into payroll costs and execution.

services.title: HMRC Compliance & RTI
subheadings: Management of PAYE, National Insurance, and all Real Time Information (RTI) submissions
paragraphs: We ensure full compliance with HMRC requirements, including Real Time Information (RTI) submissions, PAYE tax withholding, National Insurance calculations, and all statutory reporting. Our team monitors legislative changes to keep your payroll compliant.

services.title: Pensions & Auto-Enrolment
subheadings: Administration of workplace pension schemes, including enrolment, contributions and compliance
paragraphs: We manage workplace pension scheme administration, including employee auto-enrolment, contribution calculations, and ongoing compliance. We coordinate with pension providers and maintain required records and reporting.

services.title: HR Administration
subheadings: Employee onboarding and offboarding, contracts of employment, and maintenance of personnel records
paragraphs: We provide practical HR support, including employee onboarding/offboarding workflows, employment contract management, and maintenance of statutory personnel records.

services.title: Statutory Payments & Leave
subheadings: Management of statutory sick pay (SSP), maternity/paternity pay and associated entitlements
paragraphs: We calculate and process statutory payments, including SSP, maternity/paternity pay, and associated leave entitlements, ensuring compliance with employment law.

services.title: Advisory & Best Practice
subheadings: Guidance on UK employment processes, policies and operational efficiency
paragraphs: We offer advisory support on employment policy development, compliance best practice, and payroll process optimization to support your operational efficiency.

header.title: Overseas Entities & Beneficial Ownership
header.subtitle: Register of Overseas Entities (ROE) compliance and beneficial ownership verification under the Economic Crime Act 2022.

overview.title: Register of Overseas Entities (ROE)
paragraphs: The UK's Register of Overseas Entities (ROE) came into force on the 1st August 2022 through the Economic Crime (Transparency and Enforcement) Act 2022 as part of the Government's response to combat criminal use of UK property and increase transparency of overseas entities owning UK land. ROE requires any overseas company, partnership, foundation, or similar entity with a legal personality that owns UK property to register with Companies House and keep the information up to date annually.

For overseas entities, ROE registration is mandatory before acquiring or holding UK property. Failure to register incurs significant penalties, and overseas entities cannot hold property in their own name without registration. Beneficial owners of registered overseas entities must be accurately identified, with ongoing reporting obligations if beneficial ownership structures change.

We guide overseas entities through ROE registration, collect beneficial ownership information, verify identity documentation, and manage ongoing reporting obligations. Our team provides a full end-to-end service for new registrations, annual update filings, and transfers of existing ROE cases from other ACSPs to support ongoing filings.

services.title: Complete registration support for overseas owners of UK property
paragraphs: We assist overseas entities in obtaining an Overseas Entity ID (OE ID) and meeting all statutory requirements under the Economic Crime and Corporate Transparency Act (ECCTA).
subheadings: Our new ROE application service includes:
bullets: Client onboarding
Verification review to identify beneficial owner(s) or Managing officers
Verifying and complying identity checks on all registrable persons
Preparation and submission of the ROE application to Companies House and obtaining the Overseas Entity ID
Liaison with Companies House until approval
Guidance on ongoing obligations

services.title: Who needs to register?
paragraphs: You must register if your overseas entity:
bullets: Owns or acquires UK freehold property
Has a long leasehold (more than 7 years)
Plans to purchase or dispose of UK property or land

services.title: What you receive
bullets: Your official Overseas Entity ID
Compliance confirmation for property transactions
Ongoing support as required

services.title: Transfer-in service
paragraphs: If your overseas entity is already on the register but you want a new UK ACSP to manage filings or verification, we provide a smooth transfer process. Perfect if you have lost contact with your previous agent, changed ownership structure, or need a more responsive service.
subheadings: Our transfer-in service includes:
bullets: Reviewing your existing ROE submission
Identifying any compliance gaps
Managing the transition from your current agent
Completing your next verification and update
Providing ongoing support and reminders before deadlines

services.title: Annual Updating Statement
paragraphs: Every overseas entity must file an Annual Updating Statement every 12 months, even if nothing has changed. Failure to update can lead to financial penalties, prosecution, and restrictions on selling or leasing UK property.

Our annual update service includes reviewing all previously submitted information, refreshing identity verification where required, preparing and filing the update with Companies House, and confirmation of acceptance for your records. We help you keep your OE ID active and compliant year after year.

header.title: Process Agent Services
header.subtitle: Legal process representation for overseas companies operating in the UK. CPR compliance and court document service.

overview.title: Legal Process Representation for Overseas Companies
paragraphs: A UK Process Agent (also known as ASP or sometimes ASLP) is required when a party to a transaction does not have a physical presence in the UK where the transaction is taking place. A UK Process Agent can act for a company and/or individuals acting as the agent for court actions and/or arbitration proceedings, receiving notices where a UK presence is necessary for contracts under the laws of England and Wales.

The Process Agent's criteria are to accept delivery of any documents referring to court proceedings issued out of the courts of England and ensure that they are forwarded quickly to the client, to minimise delays allowing the client sufficient time to respond. We provide professional process agent services, acting as your authorized UK representative for legal service and court proceedings.

Our service ensures that all court documents, legal notices, and litigation correspondence are received promptly, secured confidentially, and forwarded immediately to your company's decision-makers. We maintain secure document handling, provide proof of service, and coordinate with your legal advisors throughout litigation processes.

Upon receipt of the completed instructions, Axsuma will set up the new facility, and email a confirmation letter and our invoice
Depending on complexity, the process takes 2-3 working days

services.title: Is your client in a hurry?
paragraphs: We provide a same-day service option for our valued clients who are pressed for time. This service incurs an additional fee, and online orders must be placed before 2pm UK time. Clients will receive their confirmation letter by the close of business on the same working day.

services.title: Any KYC requirements?
paragraphs: No, there are currently no AML requirements when Axsuma agrees to act as your process agent.

services.title: What do we charge?
paragraphs: All fees in GBP (£) exclusive of VAT.
For any combinations not covered by this matrix, please ask us for a quote.

header.title: Identity Verification Services
header.subtitle: Companies House authorised identity verification under the Economic Crime and Corporate Transparency Act. Streamlined, expert-guided verification for directors, PSCs, and company members.

paragraphs: From 18 November 2026, identity verification under the UK's Economic Crime and Corporate Transparency Act (ECCTA) became a legal requirement, initiating a 12-month transition period for compliance.

Axsuma is a Companies House Authorised Corporate Service Provider (ACSP), authorised to carry out identity verification on behalf of clients. As part of the UK Government's drive to enhance transparency and strengthen trust in corporate structures, the ECCTA requires all directors, LLP members, Persons with Significant Control (PSCs), and individuals filing on behalf of companies to verify their identity with Companies House. Upon successful verification, each individual will be issued with a unique personal code.

Where a corporate director or a corporate entity is listed as a PSC (i.e. a Relevant Legal Entity), details of at least one director of that entity must also be provided for identity verification. The unique reference code is assigned to the individual rather than their role or any specific company. As such, individuals holding multiple roles (for example, acting as both a director and PSC, or serving across multiple entities) are only required to complete the verification process once.

By choosing to verify through an ACSP, your ID documents and verification evidence are processed and stored by the ACSP rather than within the GOV.UK One Login identity system. This ensures your documentation is managed within a single regulated professional services relationship, rather than a centralised government identity platform.
We offer a streamlined, fully supported process to help you meet these requirements efficiently and with confidence. You can begin the process online, with expert guidance available at every stage.

header.title: UK Address for Service
header.subtitle: Address for Service for Intellectual Property Rights

paragraphs: We provide a UK Address for Service (UK AFS) for intellectual property matters, supporting compliance with the requirements of the UK Intellectual Property Office and use in UK IP proceedings, ensuring the effective receipt of formal notices and correspondence.

An address for service is the UK address to which the IPO or third parties may send correspondence relating to an application or registered right, and where documents in IP proceedings may be delivered.
Share your requirements via the contact form and we will connect you with the appropriate specialist.

header.title: International Expansion
header.subtitle: Coordinate your multi-jurisdictional expansion with confidence, backed by trusted local expertise and UK-led oversight.

intro.paragraph1: For over 20 years, our specialists have supported companies establishing and operating in the UK. We know that many businesses are not entering a single market but expanding across multiple jurisdictions at the same time and require a coordinated multi jurisdictional approach to expansion.
intro.paragraph2: We support clients entering new markets by combining UK led coordination with a trusted international network of local service providers. This enables us to navigate administrative, legal, regulatory and cultural complexities, and removing friction from the expansion process.

network.title: Our Network
